tldr.title: Exploit brokers pay $500k for WordPress RCEs. I found one with GPT5.6 and $25
tldr.author: infosecau
bullets: An LLM agent (cited as GPT5.6) discovered a critical WordPress RCE zero-day for just $25 in API compute costs.
Automated LLM workflows dramatically lower the technical and financial barriers to identifying complex, high-value software vulnerabilities at scale.
The extreme ROI asymmetry of AI-driven bug hunting will rapidly accelerate the deployment of offensive autonomous agent frameworks.

tldr.title: Minimal Git CI using hooks
tldr.author: klingtnet
bullets: Use native Git server-side hooks like post-receive to trigger automated testing and deployment scripts directly on push events.
Write lightweight CI pipelines in POSIX-compliant shell, eliminating resource-heavy dependencies on orchestrators like Jenkins or GitLab Runners.
Isolate execution environments by running hook-triggered build steps under unprivileged system users or within sandboxed Docker containers.

tldr.title: Fuzzing for fun - unauthenticated denial of service in snac2
tldr.author: ignaloidas
bullets: Fuzzing the C-based ActivityPub daemon `snac2` exposed an unauthenticated denial of service vulnerability in its request processing code.
The vulnerability highlights the risks of memory unsafety in C-based fediverse software when parsing untrusted, unauthenticated ActivityPub JSON payloads.
Deployers of `snac2` should apply patches; C developers should integrate continuous fuzzing using AFL++ or libFuzzer into their pipelines.

tldr.title: Airbus Takes Flight from AWS
tldr.author: bbg2401
bullets: Airbus is migrating workloads off AWS to hybrid and on-premises infrastructure, driven by public cloud costs and European data sovereignty.
Standardizing on cloud-agnostic platforms like Kubernetes and Terraform allows enterprises to execute large-scale cloud repatriation without rewriting core application code.
Repatriation forces DevOps teams to re-engineer bare-metal provisioning, physical networking, and localized security controls previously managed by AWS.

tldr.title: Beyond grep: The case for a context-rich AI coding harness
tldr.author: Samuel Axon
bullets: Augment Code's Vinay Perneti talks models, harnesses, and context.
Beyond grep: The case for a context-rich AI coding harness
Submitted by Samuel Axon on arstechnica.com.
